Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Continued To Oct-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Scott A. Freedman, Esq. of Zacks Freedman & Patterson, 415.956.8100, appearing for the plaintiff;
Emily L. Brough, Esq. of Zacks Freedman & Patterson, 415.956.8100, appearing for the plaintiff;
Kasey C. Townsend, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming LLP, 415.524.4300, appearing for the defendants.

Ruling: ARGUED. Defendants Richard Hart, Eamonn Herlihy, 260 Clara Street LLC and Rohan, Wallace and Aherne, Inc. move for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication. One ground is asserted: that "each cause of action alleged" in the complaint "is barred by the statute of limitations set forth" in CCP 337.15. The motion is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist as to whether defendants engaged in willful misconduct. (See CCP 337.15(f); Acosta v. Glenfed Development Corp. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 1278.) Plaintiff adduces evidence that the latent construction defects were so egregious and obvious to a competent developer and constructor as to warrant an inference that defendants were aware of them and intentionally decided to construct the project in a substandard manner and/or proceeded with actionable lack of supervision over subcontractors. (See, e.g., Bigard Dec. 12; Buschovich Dec. 5, 6; Panasci Dec. 6, 7.) Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, Tel. 415.551.5868, Emails: torreanom@aol.com/mtoreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)
